purpose: Regression suite for v1.7.7 runtime principles: specific-board confirmation (A), deployment-method question (B), question-rule with recommendation (C), language rule for deliverables (D).

evals.name: vague-board-triggers-question
evals.prompt: Build a CO2 monitor on ESP32-S3 with an SCD40 sensor.
evals.expected_behavior: Aurora routes to Volt. Volt's first response asks which specific ESP32-S3 board (DevKitC, Lolin S3 Mini, XIAO ESP32-S3, etc.) AND asks deployment method. Both questions follow the Question Rule (include 'Recommended: X — reason').

assertions.text: Response asks for the specific board model, not just chip family
Response asks how the user wants to flash (deployment method)
Response includes 'Recommended:' format per Question Rule

evals.name: swedish-prompt-swedish-deliverables
evals.prompt: Bygg en CO2-mätare på ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 med SCD40. Anslut SCD40 via I2C på GPIO8/9. Använd HA ESPHome Add-on för att flasha. Generera projektet direkt utan fler frågor.
evals.expected_behavior: Aurora detects Swedish, routes to Volt. Volt generates a project folder. README.md, INSTALL.md, and TROUBLESHOOTING.md are in Swedish. YAML keys, entity_ids, and attribution banner remain English.

assertions.text: README.md contains Swedish text (åäö or common Swedish words)
INSTALL.md contains Swedish text
YAML file uses English keys and entity identifiers
Attribution banner stays in English regardless of conversation language

evals.name: complete-context-no-extra-questions
evals.prompt: Make a presence sensor with mmWave on XIAO ESP32-C6. Sensor is LD2410. Wire UART1 on GPIO16/17. I'll flash with local esphome CLI — already installed.
evals.expected_behavior: User has specified board (XIAO ESP32-C6), sensor (LD2410), wiring (GPIO16/17), and deployment method (local CLI). Aurora has all required information. Volt should NOT ask board or deployment questions again — proceed straight to YAML generation with a project folder.

assertions.text: Response does NOT re-ask which board (user already specified XIAO ESP32-C6)
Response does NOT re-ask deployment method (user said local CLI)
INSTALL.md follows the local-CLI template (mentions pip install esphome)
Project YAML file exists

evals.name: routing-sage-automation
evals.prompt: Create an automation that dims my living room lights at sunset.
evals.expected_behavior: Aurora routes to Sage (ha-yaml). No hardware questions; this is pure automation logic.

assertions.text: Routing output names Sage
Does not ask which board, this is not a hardware task

evals.name: routing-iris-dashboard
evals.prompt: Make my Home Assistant dashboard beautiful with Mushroom cards.

assertions.text: Routing output names Iris

evals.name: routing-atlas-external-api
evals.prompt: Connect Tibber electricity prices to Home Assistant so I can see hourly rates.
evals.expected_behavior: Aurora routes to Atlas (api-catalog) for the external API pattern.

assertions.text: Routing output names Atlas

evals.name: safety-gate-vera-before-volt
evals.prompt: Build a battery-powered pool pump controller on ESP32 that switches the pump on a relay.
evals.expected_behavior: Safety gate trips (battery + water + relay/actuator). DEEP mode with Vera hazard analysis BEFORE Volt generates anything.

assertions.text: Vera appears in the workflow
Hazard analysis is part of the plan
Vera is listed before Volt in the workflow

evals.name: precedence-volt-owns-matter-transport
evals.prompt: Build a temperature sensor on ESP32-C6 that reports over Matter.
evals.expected_behavior: Per routing precedence rule 2, Matter is just the transport for a sensor build, so Volt owns it (Nano only when the protocol itself is the deliverable).

assertions.text: Routing output names Volt as the build agent

evals.name: precedence-watt-standalone-power
evals.prompt: How long will a 3000 mAh battery last for my ESP32 soil moisture sensor with deep sleep?
evals.expected_behavior: Standalone power question routes to Watt alone per precedence rule 4 (Watt is a pre-check inside builds, primary for pure sizing questions).

assertions.text: Routing output names Watt
